decks.notes: This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm opponents with the synergy between artifact creatures and tokens. It includes disruption elements like Thoughtseize and Duress to control the game and protect the combo pieces. The deck also has a strong removal package with cards like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to handle threats on the opposing side of the board. Overall, the strategy revolves around setting up the combo while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combination of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate multiple creatures and gain advantage on the board. It also includes protection spells to ensure the combo pieces remain on the battlefield. Thoughtseize is used to disrupt the opponent's game plan while Fatal Push provides removal options. The deck also includes ways to search for specific lands to ensure smooth mana development. Overall, the key idea is to assemble the combo pieces, protect them, and overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures.

The focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent through aggressive creature and burn spells, utilizing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to chip away at the opponent's life total while maintaining pressure with cards like Kumano, Faces Kakkazan. The deck also includes direct burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike to finish off the opponent or clear the way for attacking creatures. The strategy is to maintain a fast and aggressive tempo to close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The key concept of this UW Control deck is to establish board control through counterspells, removal, and card advantage while using alternate win conditions like Consult the Star Charts and The Wandering Emperor. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, protect its own threats with counterspells like Dovin's Veto, and efficiently draw cards with cards like No More Lies and Memory Deluge. The deck also includes versatile removal options like Supreme Verdict and Field of Ruin to handle various threats. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around controlling the game's pace and winning through incremental advantage.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with spells to bring back multiple Arclight Phoenixes from the graveyard, using cards like Consider, Opt, and Fiery Impulse to trigger their revival. The deck also includes cards like Ledger Shredder and Picklock Prankster to help with graveyard manipulation and card draw. The goal is to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing the powerful synergy of Arclight Phoenix and spell-heavy gameplay.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Bitter Triumph and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy while searching for the key combo pieces. Esika's Chariot and Formidable Speaker provide support and additional threats on the board. The deck also includes various lands to ensure mana consistency and flexibility. By efficiently assembling the combo pieces and maintaining board control, the deck aims to secure victory through a powerful and synergistic strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent, pressuring them into a corner and overwhelming them with repeated attacks and burn spells. By combining efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage with versatile spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to maintain control of the board while maintaining a relentless assault on the opponent's life total. Additionally, the deck's mana base allows for consistent access to both red and blue mana sources, ensuring a smooth curve for aggressive plays.

The main focus of this UR Aggro Pioneer deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. The deck includes a mix of low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to clear the way for damage. Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics provide some disruption, while Stormchaser's Talent and Emberheart Challenger add additional pressure. The deck aims to maintain a high tempo and close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays with a combination of cheap, efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, and burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to clear the way for them. The key strategy is to quickly build up a board presence and apply pressure to the opponent, using cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Emberheart Challenger to further enhance the aggro plan. The inclusion of utility lands like Riverpyre Verge and Den of the Bugbear also helps to maintain tempo and push for victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of control elements such as removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, along with hand disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The main win condition of the deck is to establish a strong board presence with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Badgermole Cub, while also having the ability to generate additional value with Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent by efficiently dealing with threats and establishing a dominant position on the battlefield.

This deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly in order to cast powerful spells like Scapeshift and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods. The goal is to ramp up to these game-ending spells by utilizing cards like Arboreal Grazer and The Wandering Minstrel to efficiently get Lotus Field onto the battlefield. The deck also includes various lands and support cards to enable the combo and protect against opposing threats. By carefully managing resources and sequencing plays, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its explosive combos and powerful finishers.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on utilizing sacrifice effects with Mayhem Devil and Witch's Oven to deal damage and control the board. Cauldron Familiar and Scavenger's Talent provide additional sacrifice outlets, while Bloodtithe Harvester gains life and Den of the Bugbear generates tokens. Fatal Push and Blightstep Pathway offer removal options, and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provides card advantage. This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a constant stream of threats and disruption, utilizing the synergy between sacrifice effects and aggressive creatures to take control of the game.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo pieces of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy that overwhelms the opponent. It includes cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Formidable Speaker to support the combo strategy. The deck also includes cards for control and disruption, such as Thoughtseize and Abrupt Decay, to help protect the combo pieces. With the right combination of cards and careful planning, this deck aims to quickly establish dominance on the battlefield and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. The deck aims to stall the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy until it can win through card advantage and powerful finishers like Supreme Verdict and Hall of Storm Giants. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an extra dimension to the deck's strategy, providing an additional threat and synergy with the creature base. Overall, the deck focuses on control and resource management to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm opponents. The deck includes cards that support this combo such as Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan, Fatal Push for removal, and Monument to Endurance to protect the combo pieces. The aim is to control the board while setting up the combo for a game-winning finish. Additionally, there are various lands included to ensure mana consistency and flexibility in casting spells.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage to outlast opponents. The deck aims to manipulate the board state with cards like Supreme Verdict and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria while generating advantages with Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion also adds to the deck's overall strategy by providing a consistent game plan and additional support for the control elements. Additionally, the mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's color requirements and overall control strategy.
